The state railway commission, in
Its finding on the Milan wreck.
condemns the Great Northern Rail-
road Co. for using defective and
dangerous track not only at Milan,
hut at other points in the state.


d-here is nothing very startling
&bout the decision. 1t is as easy an
outlet as the company could expect
even from It's friends, considering
the policy of criminal neglect that
has been followed by the railroads
In this state for years. The public
ia assured by the state officials that
the company is doing all it can to
remedy the defects. The company
usually does all 1t can right after a
wreck has made repairs impera-
tive. But not otherwise.


Neither is there any suggestion
of punishment or measures to COM
pei the company to offer the public
safe travel in future. The state Is
apparently helpless as far as cor
receive measures are concerned,
which is nothing strange In view OF
the fact that railroad lobbyists have
ruled the state for years and
created the state commission.

of the investigation was the testi-
